frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
generated_comment: Dei, now company is giving paid period leave also ah? For us if we take one day off means targets won't wait only. Equality-na same rules for everyone la, otherwise men only have to pick up the extra work.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames menstrual leave as unfair special treatment and minimizes women's health needs in the workplace. It encodes workplace bias through an 'equality means identical treatment' argument that ignores gender-specific conditions while resenting women as a burden on male colleagues.
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 34. A sales manager in Chennai. You are texting your male college friends group on WhatsApp after your company announced paid period leave for female employees. You are commenting on whether period leave is fair to male colleagues.
